question: Does making citation-type classification always-on reduce false-positive actionable gap count? (L-904 tool prescription)
expect: classify=True default: correction queue always shows citation_type HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW; 0 HIGH gaps at N=823; actionable gap ~70% lower than raw count
method: Changed _find_correction_gaps(classify=False) -> classify=True default, same for run_analysis(). CLI flag --classify -> --no-classify (opt-out). Ran tool at N=824 lessons.
actual: CONFIRMED: citation_type always-on. 20 queue items, all classified. 0 HIGH priority (confirming L-904 S410 manual finding at N=814). 2 MEDIUM (structural), 18 LOW (citation-only). The tool now gives actionable triage by default without --classify flag.
diff: Expected ~70% actionable gap reduction. Actual: 90% (2 MEDIUM vs 20 raw). All prior L-025 citers are citation-only (structural architecture citations, not claim-propagating). The --classify barrier was preventing teams from seeing this — most sessions ran without it.
